title: analytics — webhook namespaces
description: One OUTBOUND wire event: `analytics.event.delivered` (v1). No INBOUND webhooks — analytics providers don't call us back (their dashboards are the source of truth for delivered analytics data; we only care about our own delivery-side status).

events.name: analytics.event.delivered
events.trigger: AnalyticsEventDelivered event fires — all eligible providers succeeded.
consumers: downstream tenant-owned analytics warehouse (via webhook subscription)
cross-service consumers wanting to react to analytics delivery (e.g. an ETL that snapshots the event to BigQuery)

events.name: analytics.event.partially_delivered
events.trigger: AnalyticsEventPartiallyDelivered event fires — some providers exhausted retries with failures.

inbound.note: Analytics providers do NOT call us back. Provider-side event capture is one-way (POST from us to them). We rely on provider-side dashboards for downstream analytics; no inbound-webhook ingestion required for THIS module. Contrast with notifications-mail (which DOES ingest inbound bounce/complaint webhooks) or notifications-push (which does not either — same shape as analytics).
